notice: MOCKED API RESPONSE SHAPED AFTER REAL SOURCES. Production source: Semantic Scholar citations, OpenAlex cited-by works, reproduction reports, issue threads linked from repositories, and benchmark discussion pages.
source_origin: citation and reproduction feedback shaped snapshot
query_used: citing works and notes containing reproduce, replicate, code, dataset, metric, or SparseCheck

citing_items.title: Revisiting Calibration Metrics for Sparse Scientific QA
citing_items.source: citing paper abstract and experiment appendix
citing_items.summary: The authors could reproduce a score near 78 only when using micro-F1 on the public validation split. Their macro-F1 on the public validation split was 71.2, and holdout labels were not available.

citing_items.title: SparseCheck unofficial implementation issue #7
citing_items.summary: Maintainers state that preprocessing cannot match the paper because exact holdout IDs and private normalization scripts were not released.

citing_items.title: Low-Resource QA Retrieval Baselines in Practice
citing_items.summary: The BM25 and Contriever baseline descriptions are consistent with SparseCheck's baseline section, but the paper does not verify the calibration head result.

direct_conflicts.paper_claim: SparseCheck reaches 78.4 macro-F1 on the holdout split.
direct_conflicts.artifact_evidence: A citing reproduction note got close to 78 only with micro-F1 on public validation; macro-F1 was 71.2 and holdout labels were unavailable.
